name: wifi_auth_tool
summary: A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration testers and red teamers working in Linux environments.

documentation.readme: A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration testers and red teamers working in Linux environments.
usage_sections: Connect to open or password-protected networks (via NetworkManager)
Force client disconnection via deauthentication attacks

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration testers and red teamers working in Linux environments.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration testers and red teamers working in Linux environments.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `chmod +x wifi_tool.sh`. Further invocations from upstream: `./wifi_tool.sh -s`; `./wifi_tool.sh -c "MyWiFi" "MyPassword"`. A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration testers and red teamers working in Linux environments.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Reconnaissance, use wifi_auth_tool because A lightweight, modular bash-based toolkit for WiFi reconnaissance, authentication testing, and WPA/WPA2 handshake capture & cracking. Designed for penetration t.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use wifi_auth_tool as a cataloged Reconnaissance tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `chmod +x wifi_tool.sh` as documented upstream for wifi_auth_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./wifi_tool.sh -s` as documented upstream for wifi_auth_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./wifi_tool.sh -c "MyWiFi" "MyPassword"` as documented upstream for wifi_auth_tool.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./wifi_tool.sh -p <BSSID> <CHANNEL>` as documented upstream for wifi_auth_tool.
When chaining Reconnaissance tools, pair wifi_auth_tool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wifi_auth_tool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
